title: cystea alpina
intro: Cystea alpina, also known as Alpine Marshwort or Cyste alpine, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe, particularly in the Alps. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, relieve coughs, and soothe digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Cystea alpina is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
